The fifteenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet. Its sound differs from that of ر r by being uttered with the tip of the tongue turned upwards towards the roof of the mouth; and closely resembles that of ḍa, with which it is often interchanged. In reckoning by abjad it counts 200, the same as re. It rarely (in Hindī never) occurs as the first letter of a word, there being, so far as we have been able to discover, only one solitary vocable under this class.

def: s.m. corr. of rāwaṇ, q.v. s.m. lit. 'The Vociferator'; name of the ruler of Lankā or Ceylon, and the famous chief of the Rākshasas or demons whose subjugation and destruction by Rāmćandra, the seventh incarnation of Vishṇu, form the subject of the epic poem called Rāmāyaṇ, (he was the son of Viśravas by Kaikasī, and the half-brother of Kuvera).
more: n. prop. See راون. (It is said, that Vish̤ṇu, having an inclination for war, occasioned a Brāhmaṇ to curse his two door-keepers जय and विजय wishing that they might be born on earth three times, returning after each time to their original forms of porters to the deity. By these means, Vish̤ṇu contrived to be nearly matched on earth, so as to have a good battle. They first appeared as Hiraṇyāksh̤a and Hiraṇyakashipu; then, as Rāvaṇa and Kumbhakarṇa; and, lastly, as Sisupāl and Dantavaktra; in each of which characters they opposed the deity in different avatārs.

def: Rus. n. f. Meal mixed with buttermilk. Mane imrat lāge rābṛī, dāṅt lage na jābhṛī . Rus. Nice buttermilk and gruel mixed and boiled, to me, As nectar sweet, my teeth and jaws from labour free.
n. f. Well-boiled thick milk.
s.f. Thick milk; thickened milk; pap; a kind of porridge made of meal or Indian corn.

ety: inf. n. of ربط 'to tie, to bind'
def: s.m. Binding, connecting, uniting; connexion, bond, relation, dependence; consistency, fixity; friendship, intercourse; familiarity, practice, habit, use; (in Gram.) construction; the copula
more: n. m. ربط bound. 1. Connection; relation; dependance. 2. Intercourse ( ānā jānā ). 3. Practice ( istemāl , 1). Pāṅch baje uṭhne kā rabt hai . 4. Still; dexterity. 5. ( Gram .) The copula. rabt baṛhānā , v. a. To improve one's acquaintance; become intimate. be-rabtī , n. f. Unconnectedness; irrelevancy.
s. m. Connexion, binding, bond, friendship, familiarity, practice, habit, use; construction (in grammar) ربط دينا rabt̤ denā, To construe.
subs: (-se), To strengthen the bond of union (with), become more familiar (with), improve (one's) acquaintance (with), become intimate
(-ko), To connect, bind together, unite, strengthen, give consistency or fixity (to); to construe
or rabt̤-ẓabt̤, s.m. Intercourse; government, subjection, dependence; system, organization

def: n. m. 1. See rabt (2.) 2. System; organization. be-rabt , adj. Unconnected; incoherent ( an-mel , 6).
s.f. Connectedness, &c. (used in comp.)
an elevated place, high ground; The piece of land where the Dome of the Rock is sited in Jerusalem, called so because it is higher than its surroundings.
n. m. The first of the two months termed rabīa , the third of the Mah. year.
s.m. Make, form; workmanship, composition, &c. ( = raćnā)
more: n. f. S. रुचि Desire; liking; appetite.
s. f. 1. Desire, wish, avidity, desire of or pleasure from eating. 2. Light, lustre.
adj. i. q. بي نقصان Free from loss or defect (?).
subs: s.m. Composition (or make) and nature, &c. (see bhāʼo).

def: adj. & s.m. Making, fabricating, &c.; — maker, creator, fabricator, composer, arranger; inventor, contriver; decorator, adorner.

s.f. Making, forming, fabricating, creating, inventing, composing, preparing, making preparation; make, form, constitution, nature; arrangement, disposition, management, preparation, performance, accomplishment; fabrication, created thing, workmanship, work, composition, literary production.

ety: caus. of raćnā; — see rajhānā above for suffix ˚ānā
def: v.t. To cause to make or do; — to make, do; to set to work, set agoing; to make merry; to celebrate; to stain (the hands, feet, &c.) with ḥinna or mendhī.
more: v. a. S. रच to form. See rachnā , v. n.
(caus. of رچنا) v. a. 1. To set to work, to set a going. 2. To make, to do. 3. To make merry. 4. To stain the hands, feet, &c. with ḥinnā, &c. 5. To celebrate.

def: s.f. Staining (the hands, &c.) with ḥinnā, &c.
s.f. = rakshā, q.v. s.m. Guard, protector ( = rakshak, q.v.); — preserving, protecting; protection (see rakshā)
more: s. f. Protection, guarding, keeping, preservation, defence, patronage, safety.

def: s.m. = rakshak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Guarding, preserving, keeping, protecting, &c.; — protector, preserver, guardian, keeper, guard, defender, saviour.
more: s. m. A protector, keeper, guard, watchman, redeemer.

def: s.m. = rakshaṇ, q.v. s.m. Protector, &c. ( = rakshak, q.v.); — the act of guarding, watching, protecting, tending, taking care (of), preserving; protection.

s.m. = rākshas, q.v. adj. Of or belonging to a demon, demoniacal, fiendish, infernal; infested by demons; — s.m. An evil or malignant spirit, demon, devil, fiend, imp, goblin; — (fig.) a great eater, gormandizer; a form of marriage ( = rākshas-byāh, q.v.); — one of the astronomical yogas or divisions of the moon's path: (the Rākshas plays an important part in Hindū mythology, and is divisible into three classes; 1˚, the kind which has a demi-celestial nature and is ranked with the attendants on Kuvera; 2˚, the kind answering more to a demon, goblin, imp, or fiend, going about at night, haunting cemeteries, disturbing sacrifices and devout men, animating dead bodies, ensnaring and devouring human beings; 3˚, the kind approaching more to the idea of a Titan or relentless enemy of the gods in a superhuman or incarnate form, such as Rāvan)
more: n. m. rāchchhasnī , n. f. S. राक्षस, Pālī, rakkhaso . 1. A demon; fiend; an evil spirit. 2. A child born with horns. 3. A fabulous race with horns, now extinct. rāchchhas byāh , n. m. A marriage in which the appointed rites have not been observed; violent seizure and rape of a girl after the repulse or destruction of her relatives. rāchchhas-belā, rāchchhasī-belā , n. m. The evening twilight when evil spirits are abroad; the gloaming.
s. m. A demon, fiend, an evil spirit. راچهس بياه rachh̤as-byāh ( राक्षसविवाह ) A form of marriage, the violent seizure and rape of a girl after the repulse or destruction of her relatives.
subs: s.m. One of the eight forms of marriage (the violent seizure and rape of a girl after the repulse or destruction of her relatives)
s.f. The time when evil spirits are abroad, the period included between sunset and night, the evening twilight.

def: v.t. corr. of rakshnā; and rakhnā, qq.v.
alternative spelling of روچڈیل (raucḍel)

ety: prob. S. राक्षसः; cf. S. राक्षसी 'a large tooth'
def: s.m. A toothed instrument (as a weaver's); instrument, implement, apparatus; — the innermost or oldest portion of a tree or timber
subs: s.m. pl. Implements; utensils; — ćakkī-kā rāćh, The pivot on which a hand-mill turns.

def: adj. & s.f. = rākshasī, q.v. s.f. A female rākshas or demon; — a large tooth, a tusk; — a sort of perfume
more: 1. adj. Of or relating to a rāchh̤as, demoniacal, devilish. 2. s. f. A female راچهس rāchh̤as, q. v.

def: s.f. = rākshasī, q.v. s.f. A female rākshas or demon; — a large tooth, a tusk; — a sort of perfume
more: s. f. A female راچهس rāchh̤as, q. v.

def: n. f. Staining red with hennā or pān .

n. f. See رکشا Bhāṅt bhāṅt kī dekhī nārī, nīr bharī hai, gorī kārī, Adhar baseṅ, aur jag ko bhāveṅ, richchhā kareṅ jab nīr bahāveṅ . Riddle. Ans. Badlī clouds. Females of shapes and shades with tearful eyes, some dark, some fair attend, In air they dwell, earth loves them well, in gracious mood to earth descend.

n. m. 1. An implement; apparatus (اَله); a tool. Yoh rāchhoṅ-pūrā saë . Rus. He is skilled in the use of every tool. (He is a consummate knave). 2. The innermost or oldest portion of trees or timber. rāchh rachhīṅḍe , n. m. Utensils; pots and pans. chakkī kā rāchh , n. m. The pivot on which the corn mill turns.
part. Made, formed, fabricated, created; done; prepared, composed, compiled, written (by); arranged; strung together; adorned, decorated.

ety: rać˚ = Prk. रच्च(इ) = S. रच्य(ते), pass. of rt. रच्
def: v.n. To be made, be formed or fashioned, be created, be arranged, be composed, be invented; to be set to work, be employed, be about (a business); to be used or accustomed (to), to have to do (with, -se), to be intimate or familiar (with); to become familiar, be tamed; to keep time (in music); to predestinate; (in the following senses prob. fr. S. ranjanīyam, rt. ranj), to be stained or dyed (with red); to stain, colour; to be in love (with), to love, like; to be penetrated (with); to penetrate and remain in (as scent in clothes, &c.), to be scented; — v.t. To make, form, create, produce, fabricate, invent; to prepare, get ready, make preparation for; to contrive, plan, arrange, dispose, make arrangements for, to perform, to celebrate (a marriage, &c.); to compose, write; to string together; to work, carve; to decorate, adorn.
more: v. n. 1. To be used or accustomed to; to practise. 2. To be intimate or familiar with. 1. Jo jāhī sūṅ rach rahā, tihīṅ tā hī sūṅ kām; Jaise kirvā āk ko kahā kare bas ām ? Dohā. With whom you 're intimate with him alone you have to do, As in the Āk the worm the mango careth not to woo. 2. Bairī se bach, piyāre se rach . Prov. Flee from your foe, cleave to your Joe. 3. To be tamed; to become familiar. 4. To be stained or painted red, as with pān , or henna. 5. To be scented ( basnā ). Pyāre ko pān, ku-piyārī ki meṅhdī, Rāchegā pān, bi-rāchegī meṅhdi . Prov. Your betel from the loved one's hand to you so lovely seems, While henna on the hand you hate so odious beseems. 6. To keep time in music; to harmonize.

v. a. 1. To plan; design. Parmeshar ne jo rach/?/ so hī hogā . 2. To create; make; produce. Brahmā rachte shishṭ, pālnā Vishn kareṅ, Shiv saṅghāre; Dhan dhan Shrī Gaṅgā jī jo adham pāpiyan tāre! Brahm made the world, by Vishnu cherished, Shiv did it destroy, Bless Ganges great whose waters sinners bear to eternal joy! 3. To form; fashion; carve; paint; decorate. Bedī rachnā . 4. To string together; compose. Chhand rachnā. byāh yā kāj rachnā , To celebrate a marriage.

n. f. 1. Creation; nature. 2. Composition. Kāj racho hai tore ghar, banṛ/?/, Atar suhāg se gaṅdhī meṅdhī banṛī ke hāth lagi hai rasīlī. Shauqraṅg, subh ghaṛī, subh sāat kāj racho hai! Marriage song. Banṛā . Zafar. A marriage feast is in thy house, O bride! thy hands with henna and roses unctuous dyed: O bride! auspicious hour, auspicious moment o'er thy nuptials glad preside! rach'nī , adj. Readily yielding its color; staining. Kyā rachnī meṅhdī hai!
v. n. S. रोचन from रुच to please. 1. To be agreeable to the mind or palate. 2. To feel an appetite for; to desire.

v. n. 1. To be made, to be created, to be prepared. 2. To be set to work, to be employed, to be about (a business). 3. To stain, to colour. 4. To love, to like. 5. To keep time in musick. 6. To penetrate. 7. To predestinate. 8. v. a. To make, to form, to invent, to prepare, to compose, to perform. 9. ¶ (for रचना ) s. f. Forming, composing, creating, making, invention. 10. Created thing, work, workmanship.
v. n. To excite desire; to be agreeable.

def: v.n. To be affected or imbued (with love, or passion), to be strongly attached (to).
more: v. n. To be affected or imbued (with love), to be strongly attached.

def: s.m. Making, make, formation, &c. ( = raćnā, q.v.). v.n. To be affected or imbued (with love, or passion), to be strongly attached (to).
s.m. Splitting, rending, tearing, scraping, scratching, gnawing; a split, rent; — tooth, tusk (of an elephant, &c.)
more: s. m. A tooth. رد چهد rad-chhad ( रदछद ) s. m. The lips.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رد (radd), from Arabic رَدّ (radd).

def: s.m. Thunder; threatening words, fulmination.

ety: prob. fr. S. रध् or रध
def: s.f. Pus, matter.
s.m. = ردا raddā, q.v. s.m. Stratum (of a wall), layer (of bricks, &c.); line, row, course; the work of a day
subs: (-kā), To lay down a layer or stratum (of).

def: s.m. = rad, q.v. s.f. Pus, matter.

ety: inf. n. of ردّ 'to return, reject, repel,' &c.
def: s.m. Returning; restitution; rejection, repulsion; casting off, turning back, averting; resistance, opposition; disproving, refutation; — vomiting
more: n. m. رد returned. 1. Refutation. 1. Miyāṅ kī balāëṅ rad raheṅ! Mendicants. Evil be far from you, good Sir! 2. Jān dene pe apnī kad na kareṅ, Qai kareṅ, sab kā kahnā rad na kareṅ! Wom. Shauq. Upon his life to slay pray let him not be bent, The opium cast, but not the counsel to him lent. 2. Vomit. radd o badal, radd o qadah , n. See بحث 1, 3. radd-i-javāb , n. m. Rejoinder; refutation of a defence; reply; replication. radd-i-javāb k. , v. a. To rejoin; refute. radd-i-salām , n. Returning compliments. radd-i-qarz , n. m. G. G. Discharge or liquidation of a debt. radd k. , v. a. 1. See tardīd k . 2. To reject; pronounce unfit for service. 3. To vomit ( qaë k .). radd-i-kalām . Counter-statement; contrary declaration; contradiction; refutation. radd h. , v. n. To be refuted, abrogated, etc.

s. m. 1. Rejection, repulsion, resistance, refutation. 2. ( met. ) Vomiting. 3. Returning, restitution. ردالعجز علي الصدر raddu-l-aﻌjz aﻌlạ-ṣ-ṣadr, The antistrophe, repeating at the end of a verse the same word with which it begins, or beginning another with the word which terminated the preceding; a kind of rondeau. رد بدل radd-badal, s. f. Argument, discussion, altercation, agitation, controversy. رد جواب radd-i-jawāb, A reply, rejoinder, retort. رد سلام radd-i-salām, Returning compliments. رد کرنا radd karnā, 1. To reject, disprove, confute, refute, repel, turn back. 2. To vomit. رد هونا radd honā, To be rejected or turned back.
subs: The antistrophe (repeating at the end of a verse the same word with which it begins, or beginning another with the word which terminated the preceding); a kind of rondeau
s.m. Argument, discussion, dispute, altercation, controversy; — changing hands (as property), chopping and changing (e.g. is ʻarṣe-meṅ milkīyat-kā bahut rad-badal ho-gayā)
s.m. Reply, rejoinder, retort, refutation (of a defence); — radd-ě-jawāb karnā, To make a reply, to reply, &c.
s.m. Return of a salute; returning compliments
s.m. Repayment of a loan, liquidation of a debt
v.t. To return, refuse, reject, to turn back, repel, avert, resist, oppose, frustrate; to reply to, disprove, confute, refute; to cancel, rescind, abrogate (a law)
v.n. To be returned, be rejected, to be turned back; to be rescinded or abrogated, &c. (see rad).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رد (radd), from Arabic رَدّ (radd).

ety: fr. S. रद् 'to dig'; — Pers. رده, fr. Zend rad
def: s.m. Stratum (of a wall), layer (of bricks, &c.); line, row, course; the work of a day
more: n. m. A layer; stratum. raddā rakhnā , v. a. To lay down one layer.
s. m. A layer of a wall, the work of a day.
subs: (-kā), To lay down a layer or stratum (of).

def: adj. Rejected, thrown away, waste, used and done with; trashy, worthless, bad; pernicious, hurtful; — s.f. Anything rejected, anything worthless or unserviceable; waste; waste paper; remnants, scraps, refuse
more: adj. Rejected; thrown away; waste. rad'dī , n. f. 1. Waste paper; waste; any thing worthless or unserviceable; refuse. 2. Remnants; scraps ( chhipṭī ). raddī k. , v. n. 1. To spoil; damage; waste. 2. To throw away; consign to the rubbish basket.
adj. Rejected. s. m. Rejected things, refuse.
subs: (-kī), To make waste (of), to waste; to throw away.

ety: prob. fr. S. रध् or रध
def: s.f. Pus, matter.

s.f. Name of a celebrated cowherdess or gopī, the favourite mistress and consort of Krishna during his residence in Brindā-ban amongst the cowherds; — name of the foster-mother of Karṇa (she was the wife of Adhiratha the charioteer of king Sūra); — name of the twenty-first (or, according to some, the sixteenth) Nakshatra (containing four stars in the shape of a festoon)
more: n. f. The wife of Krishnā. Apnī rādhā ko yād karo . Prov. Remember your Rādhā? (Whenever his mistress, Kubjā , was angry with Krishnā, she would say 'go, remember your Radhā;' hence, remember your God, mind your own business, go your way).
n. prop. The name of Kr̤ish̤ṇaʼs mistress, who is also called राधिका .
subs: s.f. An epithet of Rādhā as the beloved of Krishṇ; — rādhā-kr̤ishṇ, Rādhā and Krishṇa; a kind of ejaculatory invocation much used by the devotees of Krishṇ and Rādhā
s.m. 'Rādhā's pool or well,' name of a sacred place of pilgrimage in Mathurā.

def: s.m.f.(?), A kind of silk cloth (formerly) made at Rādhā-nagar.
more: s. m. A kind of silk cloth made at Rādhānagar.

def: n. A kind of silk cloth formerly made at Rādhā-nagar .
s.f. A diminutive of the name of Rādhā (expressive of endearment); also = Rādhā, q.v.

s.m. One who rides behind another (on the same horse); — the technical name given to one of the letters of prolongation ā, ī, or ū, when it immediately precedes the (rawī, or) essential part of the rhyme (as in the words īmān, ʻus̤mān; fuẓūl, rusūl; tāwīl, tanzīl); — one or more independent words placed after the rhyme at the end of hemistichs or verses (words which ought to be the same throughout the poem), hypermeter (e.g. the word 'thee' in the following: 'There shone such truth about thee, I did not dare to doubt thee')
more: n. f. ردف to follow. lit . a man riding behind another on the same horse. The rhyming word of a poem. radīf-wār , adv. Alphabetically; according to the last letter of each stanza. ( Vile اوچها 7.).
(from ردف Heb. To follow) 1. s. m. A man riding behind another on the same horse. 2. s. f. The rhyming word of a poem. See under قافيه ḳāfiya.
subs: adv. In alphabetical order; according to the last letter of each stanza.

def: s.m. (colloq.) A cast-away, an outcast, a vagabond; a low person.
more: n. m. 1. An outcaste; cast-away; vagabond; reprobate; lost-sheep. 2. Low common people; the dregs of society. 3. An obscure person; a stranger; an intruder. Radvā-khadvā ā-ke khā gaë .

def: king; the title of officials and dignitaries, as well as the kings and royal families of former India
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀭𑀸𑀬 (rāya), from Sanskrit राजा (rā́jā, “king”). Doublet of رَاجَا (rājā).

def: n. f. رائي seeing with the eye or mind. 1. An opinion; a judgment ( bichār , 8.). 2. Counsel; advice. 3. Minute; vote. rāë pūchhnā , v. a. To consult; take advice. rāë taslīm k., yā mānnā , v. a. To accept an opinion as correct. rāë denā , v. a. To give an opinion; to vote. rāë lagānā , v. a. To pronounce judgment. rāë lenā , v. n. To ask one's opinion or vote. rāë milānā , v. a. To concur or comply with. izhār-i-rāë . An expression of opinion; a communication. mutasāvī-ul-rāë . Equally divided in opinion.
more: n. m. S. राजा 1. A prince; chief; a Hin. title. 2. A bard ( bhāṭ ). rāë , adj. Large; great; chief, as rāë jāman, Rāë-Bareilly, rāë bāṅs .

def: s. m. A Hindū title, prince, chief. راي رايان rāʼe rāyān, s.m. 1. A Hindū title, ( lit. ) The chief of princes. 2. (from राजा ) The Dīwān of the ḵẖāliṣa, or chief treasurer.
more: s. f. A kind of mustard seed with small grains. (Sinapis ramosa). راٴي کاٴي rāʼī-kā-ʼī, Broken to small pieces or atoms.

def: s. f. Wisdom, opinion, thought, notion, counsel.
n. f. A flower; Jasminumzambac .
A kind of rice.
n. m. A kind of husked rice.
s. m. A kind of spear.
s. f. A flower so called.
adj. See اکارت 1, 2. rāëgāṅ jānā yā h. , v. n. See akārat jānā
1. adj. Gratis, gratuitous, acquired without labour, useless. 2. adv. In vain.
adj. & adv. 'Found or picked up on the road'; acquired without labour; gratuitous; useless, unprofitable, bootless, vain; — gratis; in vain
subs: v.n. To be thrown away, be wasted, be lost, be in vain

s.m. Pumpkins or cucumbers sliced and pickled (in sour milk, &c.), condiment of sliced pumpkin, &c.; a salad
more: n. m. S. राजिकाक्त A salad. rāëṭā k. , v. a. To beat black and blue; to beat to a jelly.
s. m. Pumkins, &c. pickled in sour milk.
subs: (-kā), To make mincemeat (of), to beat to ribbons or to a jelly (syn. bhurtā karnā, and kaćūmar nikālnā).

def: s. m. (dakh.) Peasant, husbandman.
s.m. Raising, lifting, elevating; elevation, exaltation, promotion; taking off or away, removing, repelling; finishing, completing, settling, deciding, settlement
more: n. m. رفع put away. Settlement ( tasfīyā , 3). rafa k., rafa dafa k. , v. a. To settle or dispose of.

s. m. 1. Elevation, promotion, ascending. 2. Finishing, completing, settling, deciding. 3. Repelling, removing. رفع دفع rafﻌ dafﻌ, Deciding, settling, finishing. رفع شر raf-iﻌ-sharr, 1. Settling a dispute, deciding a difference. 2. Getting rid of a dispute. رفع کرنا rafﻌ karnā, To remove. رفع هونا rafﻌ honā, To be removed.
subs: rafʻ-dafʻ, s.m. Finishing, settling, getting rid (of), deciding; settlement, decision
s.m. Settling (or settlement of) a dispute, deciding a difference, getting rid of a dispute
(or rafʻ-dafʻ karnā), v.t. To remove, repel, obviate; to settle, dispose of, decide (a law-suit)
s.m. A deed of settlement or compromise
v.n. To be removed, be got rid of; to be settled, or decided, or disposed of.

def: s.m. Mending (a garment); cementing (broken friendship), effecting a reconciliation; pacifying, quieting; reparation; concord, agreement; peace, tranquillity.
more: (Heb. To heal; to repair) s. m. Mending (a garment), cementing broken friendship, quieting, pacifying, peace, tranquillity.

ety: for A. رفاهية, inf. n. of رفه 'to lead an easy life,' &c.

more: (from رفه To live comfortably and at ease. Heb. To be at rest, to desist) s. f. Content, affluence, enjoyment of life, repose, quiet, tranquillity.

def: A remedy. rafa h. , v. n. To be settled or disposed of. rafadād-i-hisāb . Winding up or settlement of an account.

ety: for A. رفاقة, inf. n. of رفق 'to be a rafīq,' q.v.

more: n. f. رفق accompanied. 1. Companionship; society. Marte dam tak rifāqat na chhoṛī . 2. A dherence; adhesion; loyalty. rifāqat k. , v. a. To accompany, associate or keep with; to stick to. jo na rifāqat rakhtā ho aur na khusūmat ; H. prīt aur bair donoṅ se alag , Neutral.
(prop. rafāḳat, from رفق Heb. To associate with) s. f. Companionship, society, friendship. رفاقت کرنا rifāḳat karnā, To accompany, to associate with.

def: s.f. A rifle; — a fine muslin.
more: n. f. 1. A rifle. 2. A fine muslin.

def: s.f. Pity, tenderness, compassion, commiseration; clemency, grace, favour.

def: s.f. Elevation, height, altitude; exaltation, promotion; dignity, eminence, nobility, honourableness, illustriousness.
more: s. f. Elevation, height, altitude, promotion, dignity, eminence, nobility.

more: part. act. (of رفع) 1. Elevating, exalting. 2. Repelling. 3. Carrying off.

ety: inf. n. of رفّ 'to glisten,' 'to quiver,' &c.
def: s.m. Shining, glistening, glittering; quivering, moving tremulously; winking or twinkling with the eye; swelling with moisture (herbage); defending, preserving; honouring; favouring, serving; eating much; kissing (a woman) with the tips of the lips; sucking (the lips) when kissing; sucking milk (a colt); sowing something to the lower part of a garment in order to lengthen it.
more: s. m. 1. Shining, glittering. 2. Eating much. 3. Kissing (a woman) with the tips of the lips. 4. Sucking (the lips) when kissing. 5. Sucking milk (a colt). 6. Defending, preserving. 7. Favouring, serving. 8. Honouring. 9. Swelling with moisture (herbage). 10. Winking or twinkling with the eye. 11. Moving tremulously. 12. Sewing something to the lower part of a garment, in order to lengthen it.

def: adj. High, elevated, lofty, exalted, eminent, sublime, noble, honourable, glorious; high, loud (in voice); fine, fine in texture (as cloth, &c.), delicate, thin
more: (from رفع) adj. High, sublime, elevated, exalted. رفيع الشان rafīʼuﻌ-sh-shān, A person of dignity, a nobleman of high rank. رفيع القدر rafīʼuﻌ-l-ḳadr, Of high estimation. رفيع المکان rafīʼuﻌ-l-makān, Of exalted rank.
subs: adj. & s.m. Of exalted dignity; — a person of dignity, a nobleman of high rank
adj. Of high estimation; eminent in power
adj. Of exalted station or rank.

def: n. f. Fine dust; soot.

ety: for A. رفادة rifādat, rt. رفد; by imālā
def: s.m. (orig.), 'A pad (for a horse's saddle); a pad on which bread is laid to be baked in an oven; old clouts sewed together; (ironic.) a turban.
more: n. m. رفاده rags. 1. A baker's pad. 2. ( Fac .) A turban. Kyoṅ sāhab, yeh roṭiyoṅ kā rafīdā hai, yā shekh jī kī pagṛī ? Is this a baker's pad, Sir, or a Sheikh's turban?

def: (v.n. fr. رفّ 'to shine,' &c.), s.m.? The Daurian lily, Lilium dauricum.
s.m. A companion (in travelling, and generally), associate, comrade, friend, ally; a coadjutor; an accomplice, accessory, confederate; an adherent, a follower (Pers. plur رفيقان rafīqān).
more: n. m. رفق tying together a camel's forelegs. 1. A companion; associate; ally; comrade; friend; co-adjutor. 2. A follower; dependent; adherent; partisan. 3. A confederate. rafīq-i-jurm , An accessory to a crime; accessory.
(from رفق See رفاقت) s. m. f. A friend, ally, associate, companion, comrade, accomplice, adherent. (Pers. plur. رفيقان).

def: s.m. A sect of the Shīʻas (so called because they deserted Zaid the son of ʻAlī when he forbade them to speak against the Companions of the Prophet); a member or individual of that sect, a schismatic, heretic.
more: n. m. رفض left, forsook. A heretic. A certain sect of Shias of Al kūfah, so called because they deserted Zaid the son of Alī when he forbade them to speak against the companions of the Prophet. A heretic, commonly applied by the Sunnī Mahommedans to the Shias .
(part. act. of رفض To reject) s. m. A heretick, particularly a شيعه shīʼaﻌ, q. v.

def: p. p. Past and gone; gone-by.
s.f. Going, motion (used chiefly in comp., e.g. āmad-raft, q.v.s.v. āmad); — 2˚ (contrac. of rafta, q.v.), Gone
more: (from رفتن) 1. s. f. in compos. Going. 2. contract. of رفته Gone, q. v.

ety: perf. part. of raftan, q.v.; and = S. सृप्त
def: part. Gone, past, departed; deceased, defunct; lost
more: part. pass. (of رفتن) Gone, departed, deceased, lost. (Plur. رفتگان).
subs: adv. Going on, in the act of going, in process of time; step by step, by degrees, gradually; leisurely, easily.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رفته (rafta), past participle of رفتن (raftan).

def: s.m.f. pl. (of rafta, q.v.), Those who are gone, the departed, the deceased.
s.f. Going, departure; loss.

ety: rt. Zend harp, or hrap? = S. सर्प्
def: v.n. To go, going, going away, departing; dying.
more: s. Going, departing, leaving this life.

def: adj. Fit to go; obliged to go; departing; transient.
more: adj. Fit to go, obliged to go, departing, transient.

ety: raf(tan), q.v.+tār = Pehl. tar = Zend tar = S. tār
def: s.f. Going, motion, walk, gait, pace; procedure, manner of proceeding (syn. ćāl).
more: n. f. رفتن to go. 1. See chāl (1—3). 2. Walk; pace; gait.
(from رفتن) s. f. Gait, pace, walk, going, motion, procedure.

def: adv. See āhistah , 2, 3. 1. In process of time; in due course. Rafta rafta sab ho jāe/?/gā . 2. Leisurely; easily.
more: adv. Going, in the act of going, by degrees, gradually, in process of time.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رفته رفته (rafta-rafta).

def: raft-guẕasht, perf. (of رفتن and کذشتن) Past.

ety: for A. rafwuṉ, inf. n. of رفو 'to mend; to darn'
def: s.m. Darning; darn
more: n. m. A darn. rafū chakkar meṅ ānā , v. n. To be entangled or ensnared. rafū chakkar h. , v. n. To run away; to be non est. rafū k. , v. a. To darn.

s. m. Darning, a darn. See رفا rafā. رفوچکر rafū-chakkar, Making oneʼs escape, stealing off. رفوچکرمين آجانا rafū chakkar meṅ ā-jānā, To be lost in astonishment. رفوکرنا rafū karnā, To darn. رفوگر rafū-gar, s. m. A darner. رفوگري rafū-gar-ī, s. f. The employment of darning. رفوهونا rafū honā, To run away, to retire.
subs: s.m. Making one's escape, stealing off
To be taken in the meshes (of), to be entangled (in); to be lost in astonishment
To make (one's) escape, to abscond, decamp, steal off or away
s.f. Darning; — the employment or work of darning.

def: n. m. A darner, esp. of shawls.
n. f. Darning.

s.m. A heretic ( = rāfiẓī, q.v.). s.m. A sect of the Shīʻas (so called because they deserted Zaid the son of ʻAlī when he forbade them to speak against the Companions of the Prophet); a member or individual of that sect, a schismatic, heretic.
more: s. m. A heretick. See رافضي rāfiẓī or شيعه shīʼaﻌ.

def: A deed of compromise.
s.m. The lower part or skirt of a mountain on the side of a desert; a meadow; a desert.
more: s. m. 1. A meadow. 2. A desert. 3. The skirt of a mountain on the side of a desert.

def: s.m. Colour, hue, tint, dye; mental affection, emotion, feeling, passion, love, affection, desire; joy; sorrow; anger, &c.; — a musical mode (six primary modes are enumerated, viz. Bhairav, Mālav, Sārang, Hinḍol, Vasant, and Dīpak or Megh); — music, Harmony, melody, vocal music, song, tune, air
more: n. m. S. राग 1. One of the six chief modes in music. Chhai rāg, chhattīs rāgnī hāth bāṅdhe khaṛī rahtī haiṅ . 2. Music; song; tune; air. 3. An account; a narrative; story. Tum apnā hī rāg gāte ho. rāg pūrnā , v. a. To tell a long, tedious story. rāg chhānā , v. n. To be in concert. rāg raṅg, raṅg raliyāṅ , n. m. 1. Dance and song; festivities; amusements; sports; pastimes; fandango. Bhūl gaë rāg raṅg bhūl gaë chhakṛī, Tīn chīz yād rahī, non, tel, lakṛī . Prov. The dance and song now laid aside, his bounds forgotten quite, Three things—salt, wood, and oil now present ever to his sight. (The gay bachelor sobered by the res angusta domi of married life). 2. Merry-making; revelry; revels; mirth and laughter; fun and frolic; hilarity; high jinks. 3. Jollification; saturnalia; carnival; carousals; wassail. rāg-sāgar , n. m. A song composed of many rags or musical modes. rāg gānā ; Sant. sereṅ …

s. m. (in dakh. f.) 1. A mode in musick (there being six in number), musick, song, tune. 2. Anger, passion. 3. Love. راگ چهانا rāg chhānā, v. n. To be in concert. راگ رنگ rāg-rang, s. m. Musick. راگ ساگر rāg-sāgar, s. m. A song composed of many rāgs or musical modes. راگ مالا rāg-mālā, s. f. The name of a treatise on musick (being nothing more than a collection of pictures, exhibiting the traditional history of the primary and subordinate modes, and the subjects appropriated to each).
subs: To sing a long ditty, tell a long story
(S. rāga-ćūrṇa), s.m. The tree Acocia catechu (which yields an astringent resin, and the wood of which is used in dyeing)
s.m. Love and hatred, affection and enmity
rāg-o-rang, and rāg-raliyāṅ, s.m. Music and merriment, song and mirth, merry-making, revelry, festivities, amusements, fun and frolic
s.m. A song composed of many rāgs or musical modes
To sing a song; to sing (one's own) song, tell (one's own) story (apnā being expressed, e.g. apnā rāg gāte ho)
(-meṅ), 'To bring or introduce music or song'; (fig.), to cause a quarrel
s.f. Name of a treatise on music (it is merely a collection of pictures exhibiting the traditional history of the primary and subordinate modes, and the subjects appropriated to each)

ety: = S. रक्त or रक्तक; cf. Prk. रग्गो
def: s.f. An artery, a vein; tendon, nerve, sinew, fibre; (met.) a confirmed vice; obstinacy, waywardness
more: n. f. 1. A vein; all artery. 1. Kāṅṛe ke ek rag sivā hotī hai . Prov. The one-eyed man has one vein more. (It supplies the place of sight and makes him so cunning. 2. Iskī to rag rag meṅ sharārat bharī huī hai . There is mischief in every vein of his body. 2. ( Met ). Reins ( bāg ). Inkī rag mere hāth meṅ hai . The very blood in his veins acknowledges my power. 3. (Met.) Obstinacy; waywardness. Is ko rag chaṛh rahī hai. rag utarnā , v. n. 1. To be sprained. 2. To get over a fit of obstinacy; to be appeased. rag paṭṭhā , n. m. 1. Veins and muscles. 2. Origin ( asal ). rag paṭṭhe se vāqif h. , v. n. To know one's flesh and bones, or his origin; to know one every inch. rag jālī, jāldār , ( Bot .) Reticulated. rag chaṛhnā , v. n. To be obstinate; to have one's back up. rag-dār ; H. regī , adj. Veined; veiny. rag-dār , n. m. Cloth of which the threads are uneven, so that some appear like veiu…

s. f. A vein, a fibre. رگ اترنا rag utarnā, v. n. 1. To be appeased. 2. To have a rupture. رگدار rag-dār, 1. adj. Veined. 2. s. m. Cloth in which the threads are uneven, so that some appear like veins. 3. A wicked brat. رگ ريشه rag resha, s. m. 1. Every vein, veins and fibres. 2. Every thing, all. رگ زن rag-zan, s. m. A surgeon, a bleeder, a phlebotomist. رگ چڙهنا rag chaṛhnā, To be obstinate, pettish or proud. رگ گردن rag-i-gardan, Pride. رگ مرنا rag-marnā, v. n. To lose virility, to become impotent.
subs: (-kī), To have a rupture, to have a sprained tendon, &c.; (met.) to recover from a fit of obstinacy, &c.; to be appeased
adj. Consisting of a net-work of veins, &c.; reticulated
(-ko), To have a confirmed vice; to have a fit of obstinacy, to be obstinate or mulish, to be pettish, or proud

adj. & s.m. Veined; — having a confirmed vice, hardened in vice; — cloth in which the threads are uneven, so that some appear like veins; — one who is hardened in any particular vice; a hardened wicked brat
adv. Every vein; in every vein; throughout the veins, from vein to vein
and rag-o-resha, Veins and fibres (of any living body); blood, nature, constitution; every vein and muscle; every particle, everything, all
A vein to stand out or to swell
(or rageṅ) marnā, v n. To lose virility, to become impotent
adj. Vein-like; having veins or fibres
s.f. An artery; the jugular vein.

def: s.f. = رك rak, q.v.
s.m. (in Pros.), A Cretic (¯̆¯).
more: A kind of verse. Cretic ¯ ˘ ¯ . Kellogg .

def: n. f. 1. ( Mech .) Friction; abrasion; attrition; graze. 2. The state of being worn. ragaṛ konā , ( Mech .) Angle of friction. ragaṛ khānā , v. n. 1. To be abraded, grazed. 2. To collide with; run against. ragaṛ lagnā , v. n. To be rubbed, grated, abraded.
s.f. Rubbing against, rubbing, friction, attrition, abrasion, graze; the state of being worn; — the quick beating of a drum
more: (from رگڙنا) s. f. Attrition, friction, rubbing, abrasion.
subs: (-kī), To be rubbed (by), be abraded, be grazed, be worn; to rub (against, -se), to come into contact (with), to collide (with)
(-kī), To be rabbed (by), be abraded, be grazed.

def: s.m. Rubbing, grinding; scouring, polishing; — quarrelling, wrangling; — a kind of collyrium, an ointment for the eyes
more: s. m. 1. Quarrelling. 2. Rubbing, polishing. 3. Name of a collyrium. رگڙا جهگڙا ragṛā-jhagṛā, s. m. Quarrel, debate, discussion.
subs: s.m. Debate, disputation, discussion, dissension, altercation, quarrel, wrangling, strife.

ety: ragaṛ˚, prob. = S. प्र+घृष्ट, rt. घृष्
def: v.t. To rub, rub down, grind, grate, fret, abrade, excoriate; to wear out; — to burnish, polish.
more: v. a. To rub, to scour, to grate, to fret, to abrade, to excoriate.

def: v. a. 1. To rub; scour; grate; grind. 2. To abrade; excoriate; chafe. 3. To wear out. 4. To rub against the ground; hammer; beat; punish. 5. To rub against (sexually). 1. Sāīṅ jī! tum chandan, ham pānī, jaise ragṛat bās basānī . Husband! you are as sandal wood, the water I, The more it's rubbed the more its perfumes multiply. 2. Arī ghar-sī! ragaṛ ragaṛ-kar pīs jo nānhā chūn pise! Rus. Wom. Ruined be your house! Bear strongly on the mill, so will the flour be fine!
s.m. (dialec.) Blood.
s.m. = next, q.v.
more: s. m. 1. Strong desire, avidity. 2. Wish, curiosity. 3. Esteem, affection, pleasure. رغبت کرنا rag̠ẖbat karnā, To desire, to covet.

ety: for A. رغبة, inf. n. of رغب 'to desire,' &c.
def: s.f. Desire, vehement desire, longing, avidity; wish; curiosity; inclination, disposition; esteem, affection, pleasure
more: n. f. Inclination; disposition ( chāh , 1). Afīmī miṭhās baṛī ragbat se khātā hai . Prov. Who opium eats devours sweets.
s. f. 1. Strong desire, avidity. 2. Wish, curiosity. 3. Esteem, affection, pleasure. رغبت کرنا rag̠ẖbat karnā, To desire, to covet.
subs: (-kī), To create, incite, or stimulate desire (of or for)
(-par, or -kī), To entertain desire (for), to long (for), set the heart (on)
(-kī t̤araf, or -par, or -kī), To evince desire (for), to long (for); to covet.

def: s.f. Pursuit, chase, chevy, hunt
more: n. f. 1. Pursuit; chase. 2. Breeding; pairing. Yeh kabūtar āj kal raged par hai .
subs: (-kī), To be in pursuit (of), to chase (for the purpose of pairing, — applied to pigeons and other animals).

ety: raged˚, prob. = S. प्र+खेदय(ति), rt. खिद्
def: v.t. To pursue, chase, chevy, hunt, drive off or away.
more: v. a. See ragaṛnā . To pursue; chase; follow after. 1. Hamar murugyā ke kāhe ragedle phiretā? Bhoj. 2. Are kyūṅ raged rahā saë? Rus. Why do you worry me? 3. Ke sukh saë man ne is ghar māṅ? sāsū dinoṅ ragede saë, Saukaṅr din din bheṛ kare saë, purakh kulachhṛāṅ khede saë . Rus. Wom. song. What comfort have I in this house? My mother-in-law's at me each day, My co-wife fights with me alway, My husband drives me from the house.
v. a. To pursue, chase, hunt.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A descendant of Raghu; a patronymic of Rām-ćandra; — sea, ocean; — a species of large fish, Silurus pelorius(?) — an intruding guest.
s.m. Name of an ancient king of Oude, great grandfather of Rām-ćandra; name of the offspring of this king (an abbreviation for raghu-vaṅśa, q.v.)
subs: raghu-rāj, raghu-rājā, raghu-rāʼī, s.m. = raghu-nāth, q.v.
s.m. The race or offspring of Raghu
s.m. 'The gem of the race of Raghu,' i.e. Rām-ćandra
raghu-nāyak, s.m. 'Lord of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Daśarath, and of Rām
s.m. 'Descendant or son of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām
s.m. 'The best (or chief, or the eldest) of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām
vulg. raghu-baṅs, s.m. The race or offspring of Raghu; — name of Kālidāsa's celebrated poem in honour of the race of Raghu
vulg. raghu-baṅsī, adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the family or race of Raghu; — a descendant of Raghu
s.m. 'The hero of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Coloured, dyed; imbued (with); full of passion or feeling, impassioned; passionate; loving; passionately found (of); — lustful, carnal, sensual; — a painter; a singer; a lover; a lustful man, libertine.
more: Eng. songster, n. m. 1. A singer. 2. A lover; libertine. rāg'ī , adj. Carnal; sensual.
1. s. m. A singer. 2. A lover. 3. adj. Passionate.
s. (dakh.) A sort of grain, commonly called raggy, much cultivated in the south of India. (Eleusine coracana, or Cynosurus coracanus). See منڙوي maṅṛwī.

def: s.f. A sort of grain, Eleusine coracana (much cultivated in southern India).
adj. & s.m. Self-willed, intractable, hardened, persisting in an evil or vicious course or habit; — a self-willed person; one who is hardened in any particular vice or habit.
part. adj. Desiring, wishing (for); willing; desirous; eager; curious
more: part. act. رغب Inclined to; prone to; leaning towards. rāgib k. , v. a. 1. To incline; dispose. 2. To encourage; incite to. rā'gib h. , v. n. To be inclined or disposed to.
part. act. (of رغب) Willing, wishing; (adjectively) Desirous, curious.
subs: v.t. To make (one) desirous (of), to incite, encourage; to dispose, incline (to)
(-kā), To be inclined or disposed (to); to become eager (for), desire eagerly.

def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. = ragī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Coloured, dyed; imbued (with); full of passion or feeling, impassioned; passionate; loving; passionately found (of); — lustful, carnal, sensual; — a painter; a singer; a lover; a lustful man, libertine.
s.f. (old H.) = rāṇī, q.v. s.f. Queen, princess.
s.f. (old H.) = rājnī or rāṇī, q.v. s.f. Queen, princess.

s.f. A modification of the musical mode called Rāg (thirty or thirty-six of these rāginīs are enumerated, either five or six being assigned to each Rāg, and personified as his wives); — a wanton and intriguing woman.
more: s. f. A mode in musick, the wife of a rāg (there being thirty of the rāgiṇī in number).

ety: inf. n. of رغم 'to abase'; 'to dislike,' &c.
def: s.m. Looking down, abasement, shame, reproach; contempt; dislike, aversion, repugnance, abhorrence
more: (Heb. To be indignant) s. m. 1. Looking down, shame, reproach. 2. Repugnance, abhorrence. 3. Contempt. رعم سي rag̠ẖm se, In despite.

ety: rag˚ = Prk. रग्ग(इ) or रग्गेइ), fr. रग्ग = S. रक्त, rt. रञ्ज्
def: v.n. To be attached (to), to be in love (with).
more: v. a. To be attached to, to be in love with.

def: v.n. To begin to sing; to sing.
more: v. a. To begin to sing.

def: n. m. 1. Rubbing; grinding. 2. Quarrelling; wrangling. 3. Damage; loss. 4. A collyrium; an ointment for the eyes. ragṛā jhagṛā , n. m. Dissension; argument; wrangling; strife.
adj. Quarrelsome, pugnacious, turbulent.
adj. Quarrelsome; puguacious. Mathurā ke log baṛe ragṛī, Bāṅdhat haiṅ bānkī pagṛī . Song. Mathurā men of bumptious port, Such swellish turbans they disport!
(v.n. fr. روح 'to become sprightly,' &c.), s.m.(?), Wine; — enjoying satisfaction (cf. next).

ety: Pehl. rāś, rās; Zend raithya = S. रथ्या
def: s.f. Road, way, path, passage; journey, progress; means of access, access; manner, method; custom, fashion; — postpn. By way (of, -kī); in the path (of), for the sake (of = rāh-meṅ)
subs: s.f. A present brought from a distance, or by one who has been on a journey
(-kī), To stop the path or progress (of), to refuse admittance (to), to bar out
(with -ko of person, and -kī of place), To show (one) the way (to), to guide; to show (one) the door, to turn out; to discharge, dismiss
(-kī), To come or return to the road, find the road (which had been lost); to mend (one's own) ways or manners
(-ko), To put one on the road (which had been lost); to guide (one) in the right path, to reform
To make a way or path; to establish relations (with), form an acquaintance or friendship (with)
s.f. Sweetmeats, &c. given as alms to Brāhmans or mendicants as a provision for the soul on its journey to the next world
'To seize the skirt of a wayfarer's garments,' to pick a quarrel with, quarrel (with one) without just cause
(-kī), To leave the road; to get out of the way, to give way
s.m. One who has charge of the public roads; a road-patrol; a collector of toll or duties on a road
s.f. Tolls; duties; — rāh-dārī, s.f. (local), or rāh-dārī-kā parwānā (or -kī ćiṭṭhī), s.m. A passport to be excused tolls or duties; a pass or letter to guard against molestation on the road
v.t. To show (one) a road; to make one wait (for)
(-kī), To watch or wait (for), to look (for), expect, await
(-meṅ), To open a way, make way (for, -ko); to grant access (to), to admit
or rāh-rāh-se, adv. According to custom, properly, reasonably, fairly
To continue in (one's) usual practice or mode of conduct; to behave properly or according to custom or etiquette
s.m. A traveller, wayfarer; a follower or sectary
s.f. Manners, habits, ways, customs; conduct, behaviour
s.m. Barring the way; keeping the gangway clear
To leave the right road or path, lose (one's) way, go astray; to run to excess, to exceed all bounds
v.n. To act or behave with propriety
To travel a road, get over a road; to quit a path, take a short cut
To delay or linger on the road
s.m. Road, way, passage, channel, pass; — a traveller
To take (one's own) course, follow (one's own) devices
(-kī), To take the way or road (to), to set out (for), to depart; to take (one's) way, take (oneself, apnī) off (e.g. apnī rāh lo)
To rob or plunder on the highway, to waylay; to lay (one) under restraint; to ruin (one's) prospects in life; to play false; to desert
The trace of a path to be effaced: rāh nāpnā, lit. 'To measure the road'; to walk about idly, to employ oneself unprofitably
s.m. A book or map of roads
To open out or make a road; to start or devise anything new; to devise a new mode of conduct
adj. & s.m. Ambling, ambling-paced, quick, active; — an ambling horse, a courser, steed, a good roadster
s.f. = rāh-ravish, q.v. (For other compounds see s.v. rah.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَاه (rāh). First attested in c. 1505 as Middle Hindi راہ (rah /⁠rāh⁠/).

v. n. 1. See ( ṭhairnā , 1, 3—7). 1. Sadā kisī kī nahīṅ rahī . Prov. With no one aught doth e'er remain. 2. Nekī hī rah jātī hai . Prov. Good only doth survive. 3. Masīt ḍhaë gaī, mehrāb rah gaī . Prov. The mosque come down, the arch alone remains. (A vestige only of his former greatness left). 4. De de bārūd meṅ āg! kis kī rahī hai, aur kis kī rah jāëgī? Prov. With powder blow it up! who e'er hath aught retained, with whom will aught remain? 5. Bhāt bin rah jāve, pīyā bin rahā na jāve . E. Prov. Without oṅe's rice meal one may go, without one's love not so. 6. Ghar rahe ghar ko khāë, bāhar rahe bāhar ko khāë . Prov. Who keeps within, his house doth raze; who stays without on others preys. 7. Pitu grah kabahūṅ, kabahūṅ susrārī, Rahehū jahāṅ ruchī hoë tamhārī! Rāmāyan. Or in your husband's or your father's house sometimes, live where it pleasant to you seems! 8. Sub ke kantā ghar āilo, pīarā mor kahāṅ rahilo, Jab ham sejyā sūtīla chihā chihā-ke uṭhīlā . Mag. song. Lāonī Back home is each one's spouse, where is my love delayed? I with a start wake oft while sleeping on my bed. 9. Agar dil bhar gayā Rangīṅ kā mujh se, Tau, aë anna! rahī phīr maīṅ kāhāṅ kī! Wom Rangīṅ. If Rangīṅ should grow tired of me, Ah! where, Oh nurse! should I then be! 2. To be; to exist; subsist. Mūṅh rahte, nāk se khāë . E. Prov. A mouth he has, yet with his nose he eats! (Hands for work and living on others!) 3. To remain; to be left. Kuchh tumhārā rah gayā hai . 4. To live; pass one's days. Kyoṅ bhaī! kis tarah rahe ? 5. ( Demīmonde ) To live or cohabit with a man. 6. To be in the family way. Peṭ rahnā . 7. To be stunted, benumbed, paralysed; to lose the use of (a limb, etc.), 1. Hāth rah gayā . 2. Darakht baṛhte baṛhte rah gayā. rah'te , adv. During the life time of; in the presence of; before. rah jānā , v. n. To tarry or remain behind. 2. To be left unfinished or undone. 3. To be omitted. 4. To fail. rah, rah! intj. Stop! hold! enough! rah rah-ke , adv. Again and again. Uske jāne se yeh dil meṅ āë hai rah rah-ke, hāë! Sab jahāṅ bastā hai, ek apnā hī ghar vīrāṅ huā . The widow. Since he hath gone, alas! to me disconsolate The world a garden seems, my house is desolate. rahā sahā , adj. Preserved; escaped. rahā sahā , n. m. Savings ( bachat , 1.). rahnā, sah'nā ; Sant. berel , v. n. To take up one's abode; to live in a place. rahan-hār, rahne-vālā, rahvaiyā , n. m. An occupant; inmate ( bāshindah ). Kyā re koyal kā mārnā bāgoṅ kī rahne-har? Māro harāmī sūgnā kutar kutar phal khāë! Slay not the cuckoo, native of the garden gay!
